hero.headline: Amity Online MBA in Hospitality Management: Syllabus, Fees and Career Outcomes
hero.sub: UGC-DEB approved specialisation. Spec subjects across Sem 3-4. NAAC A+, NIRF #22.

sections.tldr: Amity University Online MBA in Hospitality Management targets hotel, travel, and food service professionals seeking management credentials covering hospitality operations, revenue management, guest experience, and tourism strategy. NAAC A+, NIRF #22 at Rs 1,99,000. Best for hospitality professionals targeting hotel general manager, revenue management, or food service leadership roles.

about.body:
Hospitality Management is a specialisation for professionals in hotels, resorts, travel and tourism, food service, and events management who want to formalise their expertise with a management degree and move into senior hospitality leadership roles. The sector in India is undergoing significant expansion as domestic tourism grows, international hotel brands accelerate India entry, and food service chains scale across tier-1 and tier-2 cities.

The Amity Online MBA in Hospitality Management provides the management framework that hospitality professionals, who are often promoted on operational expertise, need to operate effectively as General Managers, Revenue Managers, or VP Operations. It covers hospitality operations strategy, guest experience management, revenue management, tourism policy, and food service operations management.

Amity University Online is NAAC A+ accredited with NIRF #22 University ranking for 2025. The degree carries the Amity University name identical to the campus degree. EdifyEdu verifies accreditation data independently and does not receive commission from Amity. Full programme overview at /universities/amity-university-online/mba.

syllabus.note: EdifyEdu has not yet received the detailed Sem 3-4 subject list for this specialisation. Contact Amity Online directly for the current curriculum before enrolling.

whoHires.body:
Hospitality Management MBA graduates from Amity are hired at hotel chains, resort groups, travel companies, airlines, food service companies, and hospitality consulting firms. Entry-level management roles: Front Office Manager, Food and Beverage Manager, Revenue Analyst, and Hospitality Operations Coordinator.

Mid-level roles: Hotel General Manager (small properties), Revenue Manager, Area Food and Beverage Manager, Hospitality Operations Manager, and Travel and Tourism Management Executive. Senior roles include VP Operations at hotel chains, Director of Revenue Management, General Manager at large hotels, and Head of Hospitality at integrated resort developments.

Key hiring organisations include Taj Hotels (Indian Hotels Company), Marriott International India, ITC Hotels, The Oberoi Group, Hyatt India, Radisson Hotel Group India, OYO (large-scale hotel chain management), Lemon Tree Hotels, Sterling Holiday Resorts, Cox and Kings, Thomas Cook India, Zomato food service operations, Swiggy Restaurants, and quick service restaurant chains (McDonald's, Dominos, KFC operations management).

skills.body:
The Hospitality Management specialisation builds skills tested at hotel operations and revenue management leadership levels:

Revenue Management: RevPAR optimisation, dynamic pricing, yield management systems, and OTA channel management — the core technical skill for hotel revenue managers. Hotel Operations Management: front office, housekeeping, and F&B operations coordination, SOPs management, and multi-department P&L responsibility at general manager level. Guest Experience Management: service design, complaint resolution systems, guest loyalty programme management, and NPS measurement in hospitality contexts. Food Service Operations: restaurant management, kitchen operations, cost management, HACCP compliance, and food service chain expansion management. Tourism and Travel Management: tourism policy understanding, destination management, tour operations, and travel technology platforms for booking and inventory management. Hospitality Marketing: hotel distribution channels, OTA strategy, social media reputation management, and direct booking conversion optimisation.

Soft skills include managing multi-cultural guest interactions, leading large hospitality teams across shifts, and presenting hotel performance metrics (occupancy, ADR, RevPAR) to ownership groups.

comparisons.body:
Comparing Amity Online MBA Hospitality Management against peers:

Amity vs IGNOU MBA Tourism and Hospitality (/universities/ignou-online/mba): IGNOU offers specialised tourism and hospitality management programmes. IGNOU is the largest distance education institution in India. For hospitality professionals, IGNOU's brand is well-recognised within the sector. Amity NIRF #22 gives it an edge for MNC hotel chain management roles that weigh university ranking in promotion criteria.

Amity vs Chandigarh University Online MBA (/universities/chandigarh-university-online/mba): CU does not offer a dedicated Hospitality Management spec in its online MBA. For hospitality professionals specifically, Amity's dedicated spec is clearly differentiated from a generic management MBA.

Amity vs Amity General Management MBA (/universities/amity-university-online/mba): General Management is broader and more recognised across sectors. For hospitality professionals who may want to transition out of the sector, General Management is more portable. For those committed to hospitality careers, the dedicated Hospitality Management spec signals sector commitment and covers relevant content more directly.

fees.body: The total fee for the Amity University Online MBA in Hospitality Management is approximately Rs 1,99,000. This includes a one-time registration fee of Rs 2,500 and four semester fees of Rs 49,750 each. EMI options start from Rs 8,292 per month. Examination fees may apply separately. EdifyEdu reconfirms fees each quarter. Always verify the current fee at the official Amity Online portal before making any payment. Fees listed are indicative.

certificate.body: The Amity University degree certificate carries the Amity University name and the MBA designation. The specialisation (Hospitality Management) is noted on the marksheet and transcript. The certificate does not display the word online on the face, consistent with UGC-DEB norms. For hospitality professionals working at international hotel chains, a management degree from a NAAC A+ university with NIRF #22 ranking is a recognised qualification for GM and Director-level roles.

reviews.intro: Three verified reviews from Amity Online MBA Hospitality Management alumni. Ratings reflect individual experience.

items.name: Aarav Sharma
items.liked: Revenue management module with dynamic pricing and OTA channel strategy was exactly what I needed for my hotel role
items.disliked: Event management content was limited — MICE (meetings, incentives, conferencing, exhibitions) is a major revenue driver at hotels
items.body: I was a front office manager targeting a revenue manager role. The revenue management and hotel operations strategy content gave me the frameworks I needed. My ADR and RevPAR targets improved 18% after applying the yield management approaches from this module. The Amity MBA was a requirement for the Revenue Manager role at the international chain I joined. NIRF #22 was specifically noted by the HR recruiter.

items.name: Divya Nair
items.liked: Tourism and destination management content was particularly relevant for resort and travel operations in India
items.disliked: Sustainable tourism and eco-resort management content was minimal — growing area for resort development in India
items.body: I manage operations at a beach resort. The hospitality operations and guest experience management content was substantive and gave me management frameworks I was applying intuitively. The Amity degree helped me move from Operations Manager to General Manager of a smaller property. The NIRF ranking was accepted by the hotel ownership group as a strong management qualification.

items.name: Kiran Joseph
items.liked: Food service operations content was thorough — HACCP and food safety compliance are increasingly required at hotel F&B management level
items.disliked: International hotel management content focused more on global chains — more content on Indian hospitality chains would be useful
items.body: I manage the F&B division at a mid-size hotel. The food service operations, cost management, and compliance content was directly applicable. The Amity MBA credential helped me qualify for the F&B Director role at a larger property that required a management degree. The NAAC A+ and NIRF #22 combination was sufficient for the international hotel chain's credential verification process.

faqs.question: Is a hotel management diploma equivalent to or better than this MBA?
faqs.answer: A hotel management diploma (IHM Pusa, IHM Mumbai) is operationally focused and industry-recognised for entry-level and mid-level hospitality roles. The Amity MBA is a management degree from a NAAC A+, NIRF #22 university that provides the business management context for senior leadership roles. For General Manager, Revenue Manager, or Director-level positions at large hotel chains, the MBA is typically required alongside or above a diploma. Both credentials serve different purposes.

faqs.question: Is this MBA useful for professionals wanting to move from hotels to airline or travel management?
faqs.answer: Yes. The hospitality management framework, service design, and operations management content applies broadly to the travel and tourism sector including airlines, cruise operations, and travel companies. The management foundation from Sem 1-2 gives you cross-sector credentials. Many hospitality professionals use this MBA to qualify for operations management roles at airlines, travel aggregators, and tourism boards.

faqs.question: What are the Sem 3-4 subjects for Hospitality Management at Amity?
faqs.answer: Detailed subject information for this specialisation has not yet been published on the EdifyEdu platform. Contact Amity Online directly for the current Sem 3-4 curriculum. Verify that subjects cover the areas most relevant to your target role (revenue management, food service, tourism, or hotel operations) before enrolling.

faqs.question: What salary can Hospitality Management MBA graduates expect?
faqs.answer: Hotel General Managers at mid-size properties (50-150 rooms) typically earn Rs 8-18 lakh. Revenue Managers at larger chains earn Rs 10-20 lakh. F&B Directors and Operations Directors at large hotels earn Rs 12-25 lakh. VP Operations at hotel chains pay Rs 20-40 lakh. Outcomes depend on hotel category (5-star vs budget chain), city, and ownership group (Indian vs international brand).

faqs.question: Can I do this MBA while working rotating hotel shifts?
faqs.answer: Yes. The online programme is specifically designed for working professionals with irregular schedules. Weekend live sessions and 24/7 recordings mean you can attend and review sessions outside your shift hours. Online proctored examinations require no campus visits. Hospitality professionals with shift work are a common cohort in this programme.

chooseIf: You are a hospitality professional — hotel operations manager, F&B manager, revenue analyst, or travel executive — targeting General Manager, Revenue Manager, or Director-level roles.
You work in hotels, resorts, food service, or tourism and want a formal management credential covering hospitality operations, revenue management, and guest experience at a NIRF #22 university.
You need a management degree to qualify for senior hospitality roles at international hotel chains or large domestic hospitality groups.
consider: You want a more portable credential that allows you to switch from hospitality to other sectors — General Management at Amity is more widely recognised outside hospitality.
You are targeting premium hospitality consulting or brand strategy roles — a NMIMS or Symbiosis MBA carries broader recognition in consulting hiring.
You are early in your hospitality career without operational experience — a hotel management diploma combined with on-the-job experience may be more effective before pursuing an MBA.
